text.p: The State Emergency Service advises that people should:
* Don't drive, ride or walk through flood water.
* Keep clear of creeks and storm drains.
* Stay indoors, away from windows, while storms are nearby.

warning-info.text: The next warning is due to be issued by 4:25 am.

text.p: Check https://www.bom.gov.au/weather-and-climate/warnings-and-alerts. Warnings are also available through TV and Radio broadcasts or call 1300 659 210. The Bureau and State Emergency Service would appreciate warnings being broadcast regularly.

forecast-period.text: Heavy rainfall about southern Yorke Peninsula

text.p: Weather Situation: Showers and thunderstorms are moving across southern Yorke Peninsula producing heavy rainfall.
A separate Severe Weather Warning is current at the time of issue.
Severe thunderstorms are likely to produce heavy rainfall that may lead to flash flooding in the warning area over the next several hours. Locations which may be affected include Warooka.
22mm at Stenhouse Bay in one hour to 12:45am.
Severe thunderstorms are likely to produce heavy rainfall that may lead to flash flooding in the warning area over the next several hours. Locations which may be affected include Warooka.
22mm at Stenhouse Bay in one hour to 12:45am.
